ddls:
CREATE TABLE "movies" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each movie */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the movie */,
  "release_year" INTEGER /* Year the movie was released */,
  "director_id" INTEGER /* ID of the director of the movie */,
  "language" TEXT /* Language of the movie */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of origin of the movie */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Duration of the movie in minutes */,
  "budget" REAL /* Budget of the movie */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Revenue generated by the movie */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline or slogan of the movie */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie poster */,
  "trailer_url" TEXT /* URL of the movie trailer */,
  "production_company" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_movies_director_id FOREIGN KEY ("director_id") REFERENCES directors ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "directors" (
  "director_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each director */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the director */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the director */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the director */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the director */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("director_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_genres" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* ID of the genre */,
  "primary_genre" INTEGER /* Indicates if the genre is the primary genre of the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "actors" (
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each actor */,
  "name" TEXT /* Name of the actor */,
  "birth_date" TEXT /* Birth date of the actor */,
  "nationality" TEXT /* Nationality of the actor */,
  "biography" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "movie_actors" (
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie */,
  "actor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the actor */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the actor in the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("movie_id", "actor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "watched_history" (
  "watched_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each watched event */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie watched */,
  "watched_date" TEXT /* Date the movie was watched */,
  "imdb_link" TEXT /* IMDb link of the movie */,
  "watched_location" TEXT /* Location where the movie was watched */,
  "watched_device" TEXT /* Device used to watch the movie */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("watched_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_watched_history_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "movie_id" INTEGER /* ID of the movie rated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who rated the movie */,
  "rating" REAL /* Rating given to the movie (out of 5) */,
  "review" TEXT /* Review of the movie */,
  "review_date" TEXT /* Date the review was written */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_ratings_movie_id FOREIGN KEY ("movie_id") REFERENCES movies ("movie_id")
)
column_names: ID of the director of the movie
Country of origin of the movie
Duration of the movie in minutes
Tagline or slogan of the movie
Indicates if the genre is the primary genre of the movie
Role of the actor in the movie
Unique identifier for each watched event
Location where the movie was watched
Device used to watch the movie
ID of the user who rated the movie
Rating given to the movie (out of 5)
